extraction.one_line_summary: JACOBSEN Fabio won headwind sprint after crashes decimated field, positioning saved by teammates after Lampaert's crash necessitated leadout scramble
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step controlled despite yellow jersey Lampaert crashing, demonstrating team depth can compensate for leadership chaos late-stage
Crosswind bridge threat neutered by headwind shift at 30km, revealing weather forecasts alone insufficient for positioning urgency
EF Education committed extended resources recovering Uran from mechanical past 10km mark, exposing GC team priority over sprint
Multiple crashes sub-3km eliminated sprint trains, favoring individually strong sprinters over leadout-dependent finishers like Ewan

rider_signals.JACOBSEN Fabio: positive: survived headwind sprint without leadout after Lampaert crash, showing individual sprint power beyond pure speed
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: finished second despite no Quick-Step coordination issues, confirming sprint versatility without dedicated train
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: suffered incident and checked sprint when leadouts disrupted, exposing dependence on structured approaches
rider_signals.URAN Rigoberto: neutral: required massive team expenditure for mechanical recovery inside 10km, questioning GC viability if repeated
rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: positive: provided non-team assistance to Uran recovery, unusual cross-team cooperation under nervous conditions
